title: Pellionia janowskyi
intro: Pellionia janowskyi is a type of plant species, commonly known as the "heart-leaf pellionia" or "ivory heartleaf," belonging to the family Urticaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pellionia janowskyi include flavonoids, glycosides, and other phenolic compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pellionia janowskyi is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be found in the form of capsules or tablets, making it easily accessible for those seeking its health benefits.
taxonomy: Pellionia janowskyi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. It belongs to the genus Pellionia and is native to New Guinea. The family Urticaceae is a group of plants commonly known as the nettle family. Pellionia janowskyi is often found growing in shaded, humid environments. This plant is a type of flowering perennial that produces small, white or greenish flowers.
